page: Google Cloud Platform g1-small is a shared-core virtual machine instance featuring 1 vCPU allocated from a shared physical core and 2.0 GB of system memory. Powered by an Intel Xeon processor running at 2.3 GHz on the x86_64 architecture, this instance utilizes a single thread per core. The CPU configuration includes a 32 KB L1 data cache, a 0.25 MB L2 cache, and a 45 MB L3 cache. It supports hardware-assisted virtualization, AVX, AES cryptography, and FMA instructions, but does not include local NVMe storage or bundled GPUs.

Performance benchmarks place this instance in the lower tiers of compute and memory capabilities. The stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU tests both yield a score of 1076.89, reflecting its shared-core limitations. Memory bandwidth is measured at 80.25 GB/sec for cached small blocks, dropping to 10.49 GB/sec for uncached large block reads and 8.91 GB/sec for writes. Passmark testing reports a CPU score of 1263.03 and a memory score of 792.89. In application-level benchmarks, the instance achieves 0.59 million operations per second in Redis SET tests and a static web serving throughput of 0.54 GB/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the g1-small offers a highly economical entry point for workloads that do not require continuous or intensive CPU utilization. Because resources are shared, it is not suitable for sustained high-performance computing, database hosting, or large-scale LLM inference. Instead, this instance is best suited for lightweight, intermittent tasks such as basic web serving, development environments, small-scale testing, and simple background automation scripts.
description: Google Cloud Platform g1-small is a shared-core virtual machine designed for entry-level, budget-conscious workloads. It features 1 vCPU on a shared Intel Xeon physical core running at 2.3 GHz, paired with 2.0 GB of RAM. Operating on the x86_64 architecture, the instance lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs. Benchmark results indicate limited performance, with a Passmark CPU score of 1263.03 and a static web serving throughput of 0.54 GB/sec. Memory bandwidth is constrained, reaching 10.49 GB/sec for uncached large block reads. Given these resource constraints and its shared-core architecture, the g1-small is best suited for low-intensity applications, including lightweight web serving, microservices, software development testing, and basic administrative scripting.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform g1-small is a shared-core virtual machine featuring 1 vCPU, 2.0 GB of RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, designed for low-intensity workloads and budget-friendly development environments.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform g1-small is a shared-core instance with 1 vCPU and 2.0 GB RAM, ideal for lightweight tasks and testing.
tagline: A budget-friendly, shared-core virtual machine designed for lightweight development, basic testing, and low-intensity background tasks.

Intel Xeon processor running at 2.3 GHz on x86_64 architecture
No local storage or dedicated GPU resources included
